texto: Children need to see themselves in books. They need to see their gender. They need to see their color, hair texture, their disability, themselves. Picture books are like many children’s first introduction to the world. Seeing yourself is almost like a message. It’s saying, you matter, you are visible, and you’re valuable
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Christian Robinson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia. O trecho “Children need to see themselves in books. They need to see their gender. They need to see their c…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, artist, equality pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e equality.
